concerns.text: FR-001 mandates downloading 'Materials Project data' in addition to the MPEA database. However, no User Story (US-1, US-2, or US-3) explicitly requests or describes the ingestion of Materials Project data. US-1 only mentions 'public repositories' generally. This creates an orphaned requirement (scope creep) as there is no story backing the specific inclusion of Materials Project data.

FR-004 requires a 'stratified 80/20 train-test split based on binned compositional ranges'. While US-3 mentions a 'holdout test set', it does not specify the stratification logic (compositional ranges vs. yield strength). The specific requirement to stratify by 'compositional ranges' is not explicitly anchored to a story's acceptance criteria, creating a potential orphaned requirement or a mismatch with the implied story scope.

FR-003.1 mandates 'Isometric Log-Ratio (ILR) or similar log-ratio transformations'. US-2 (Compositional Feature Engineering) lists specific descriptors (δ, VEC, etc.) but does not explicitly mention ILR transformation or the handling of compositional closure. While US-2 covers 'derived compositional descriptors', the specific requirement for ILR transformation is not clearly anchored to a story's acceptance scenario, risking an orphaned requirement.
